content:
Title
* Will there be a LVL 2, LVL 3, etc?

Input
* Next n lines: Please specify "x and y" in the description, since the variable names a and b don't clearly indicate which values they represent.

Constraints
* Please specify constraints for all variables, and include both the lower and upper limits, e.g.
-10000 ≤ [[x]], [[y]], [[a]], [[b]] ≤ 10000
1 ≤ r ≤ 10000
1 ≤ n ≤ 50

Stub generator
* Please add a write statement, e.g. write answer.

Cases
* Each test and its validator should form a pair and cover the same scenario with slightly different inputs (of similar magnitude) and outputs. Please adjust the cases accordingly.

* Please use a balanced range of random values as inputs.

Thanks.

Thank you so much for your feedback, as this is my first time making a contribution! As for the title, I actually do plan on making a LVL 2 or maybe even a LVL 3 version of the concept of TD Targeting, requiring more computations as difficulty increases. So, I kind of want to keep LVL 1 in the title if that's okay with you
